contextBlurb: Prince Edward Island's housing policy is guided by the Building Together – PEI Housing Strategy 2024-2026, with the 2026 Capital Budget allocating $128.3 million for affordable public housing and a total of $34.6 million in housing supports including rent supplements and mobile rental vouchers. The province operates programs such as the Affordable Housing Development Program, which provides forgivable loans of up to $55,000 per unit, and partners with the community housing sector on acquisitions and new construction. A CMHC-PEI Three Year Action Plan (2026-2028) further outlines federal-provincial investments in affordable housing access.

legislation.title: Housing Corporation Act – Funding Agreement with PEI Community Housing Development Inc.
legislation.summary: An Order in Council under the Housing Corporation Act establishing a funding agreement with PEI Community Housing Development Inc. to support community-based housing development.

legislation.title: Building Together – Prince Edward Island Housing Strategy 2024-2026
legislation.summary: A multi-year provincial housing strategy focused on accelerating housing supply, affordability, and infrastructure, with progress reported annually.
